Title: Alfalfa Transcription Factor MsNAC2a Orchestrates the Homeostasis of Salt Stress Responses via Reactive Oxygen Species Accumulation and Hydrogen Sulphide Depletion

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes salt stress tolerance and reduces oxidative damage under salinity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream biosynthetic step supplying endogenous H2S
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key H2S biosynthesis enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: downregulated in MsNAC2a-OE and upregulated in MsNAC2a-RNAi under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: analyzed in alfalfa leaves under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 500 mM NaCl; exogenous NaHS in related rescue context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes endogenous H2S accumulation and enhances salt tolerance when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsLCD1 transcript is directly repressed by MsNAC2a under salt stress. 2. MsNAC2a binds the MsLCD1 promoter by ChIP-qPCR, Y1H, dual-LUC, and EMSA. 3. MsLCD1 overexpression increases H2S content, fresh weight, and Fv/Fm and decreases electrolyte leakage under salt stress. 4. Silencing MsLCD1 in MsNAC2a-RNAi background increases salt sensitivity, placing MsLCD1 downstream of MsNAC2a.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising engineering target to increase salinity tolerance through elevating endogenous H2S; could be stacked with repression/editing of MsNAC2a
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression; VIGS silencing in MsNAC2a-RNAi background; qRT-PCR; H2S measurement; salt tolerance phenotyping; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text once mentions SlLCD1 in Section 2.6, evidently a typographical error for MsLCD1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: higher endogenous H2S supports salt tolerance; MsOASA1 contributes to H2S metabolism/detoxification reducing H2S balance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: functions as a key enzyme involved in H2S metabolism/detoxification, thereby influencing endogenous H2S homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with or reduces target-product pool through metabolism/detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key gene in H2S metabolism according to paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated in MsNAC2a-OE and downregulated in MsNAC2a-RNAi under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: analyzed in alfalfa leaves under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased MsOASA1 expression is associated with reduced endogenous H2S and decreased salt tolerance in the MsNAC2a pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsOASA1 is directly activated by MsNAC2a under salt stress. 2. MsNAC2a binds the MsOASA1 promoter through TAGC cis-elements as supported by ChIP-qPCR, Y1H, dual-LUC, and EMSA. 3. MsOASA1 upregulation correlates with H2S depletion and salt-sensitive phenotypes in MsNAC2a-OE plants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for reducing H2S catabolism/detoxification via editing or downregulation to improve salt tolerance, especially together with MsNAC2a suppression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; transgenic MsNAC2a OE/RNAi phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct functional transgenic manipulation of MsOASA1 itself was not reported in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports salt stress tolerance and redox homeostasis under salinity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor with conserved NAC DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms an interaction module/complex with MsEREBP1 in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses MsLCD1 and MsCOX11; activates MsOASA1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: H2S homeostasis and ROS scavenging during salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation and repression via promoter binding to TAGC cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: salt stress (NaCl); H2S donor NaHS; ABA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible H2S-responsive feedback because NaHS induces MsNAC2a expression, but formal feedback loop not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative regulator of endogenous H2S accumulation and salt tolerance under salinity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root, stem, leaf, and flower; highest in leaf; strongly induced by salt, NaHS, and ABA; peaks at 6 h under NaCl
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: leaf- and root-responsive to salt; promoter activity induced by salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl for expression assays; 500 mM NaCl for stress phenotyping; NaHS; ABA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and GFP fusion; native MsNAC2a promoter used in GUS assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression reduces H2S and ROS-scavenging capacity, increases ROS accumulation, and impairs salt tolerance; silencing/RNAi enhances salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsNAC2a is a salt-induced nuclear NAC transcription factor with transactivation activity. 2. Overexpression causes salt hypersensitivity, elevated ROS, reduced antioxidant enzyme activities, and decreased endogenous H2S. 3. RNAi/VIGS silencing improves salt tolerance without obvious growth penalty under normal conditions. 4. MsNAC2a directly represses MsLCD1 and MsCOX11 and activates MsOASA1. 5. Exogenous H2S rescues the salt-sensitive phenotype of MsNAC2a-OE plants.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no obvious growth or developmental penalty under normal conditions reported for RNAi/silencing; overexpression increases salt susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable overexpression; RNAi; VIGS; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation; physiological assays; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; dual-LUC; EMSA; NaHS rescue
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: previous transcriptome data cited for salt induction of MsNAC2a; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value breeding target for improving salinity tolerance through knockdown, RNAi, or genome editing to relieve repression of H2S biosynthesis and ROS-scavenging genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as both transcriptional activator and repressor depending on target promoter context; selected as core gene over homolog MsNAC2b because MsNAC2a showed stronger salt responsiveness.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: physically interacts with MsNAC2a in a nuclear protein complex/module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MsNAC2a-regulated promoters of MsLCD1; MsOASA1; MsCOX11 indirectly through MsNAC2a complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: attenuates MsNAC2a-mediated repression of MsLCD1 and MsCOX11 and attenuates MsNAC2a-mediated activation of MsOASA1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: modulation of H2S homeostasis and ROS-related transcription via MsNAC2a
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction modulating transcriptional activity, without altering MsNAC2a DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator of salt tolerance that buffers MsNAC2a negative effects on H2S homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: silencing MsEREBP1 in MsNAC2a-OE plants further increases salt sensitivity, lowers H2S, and raises oxidative damage, indicating a positive role in salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsEREBP1 is induced by salt stress. 2. MsEREBP1 physically interacts with MsNAC2a in yeast, in vivo Co-IP, and BiFC. 3. MsEREBP1 modulates MsNAC2a transcriptional activity on MsLCD1, MsOASA1, and MsCOX11 without affecting MsNAC2a promoter binding. 4. Silencing MsEREBP1 in MsNAC2a-OE background aggravates salt sensitivity and reduces H2S content.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Y2H; Co-IP; BiFC; dual-LUC; ChIP-PCR; VIGS in MsNAC2a-OE background; salt tolerance phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate positive regulator for engineering salinity tolerance, potentially through maintaining MsEREBP1 activity together with MsNAC2a suppression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts mainly by influencing MsNAC2a transcriptional activity rather than its DNA-binding ability.
